title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Baring informs Hercule Poirot that because Hubert Litcham Rock left no heirs, the Litcham Rock estate may be inherited by the deceased's nephew, the son of Hubert's sister, who may also change his surname. Poirot then speaks with Mrs. Litcham Rock, who attributes the family's recent troubles to a curse symbolized by a mirror and notes that Hubert had become increasingly eccentric.

Poirot questions the butler, Digby, regarding the timing of the gunshot. Digby states that Mr. Dahlhausen, Miss Ashby, and Mr. Keene were in the hall when the shot was heard, while Mrs. Litcham Rock, Diana Clifes, and Mr. Baring arrived later. Poirot then interviews Diana Clifes, who describes Hubert as a man of supreme authority and mental instability. She expresses indifference toward his death, noting his involvement in bad investments, though she admits she liked him. Diana also mentions she was in the garden when the shot occurred.

Poirot confronts Jeffrey Keene about picking up an object near the study door. Although Keene initially denies it, he eventually admits to possessing a tiny silk rosebud, which Poirot identifies as having come from Diana's purse.

Finally, Major John Marshall arrives and expresses shock at the suicide. Poirot questions him regarding financial irregularities and Marshall's plan to elope with the ward, Daisy. Marshall confirms the relationship, noting that Hubert was unaware of it and that Daisy is handling the situation. Marshall also mentions he is seeking new employment to avoid losing his position if the truth of the elopement is discovered.
